title: Plot likelihood scans for many POIs

posts.name: Georgios Melachroinos
posts.text:
Dear Experts,
for the BPH-24-005,  I am working on some fits with many POIs that are all measured simultaneously. I am trying to scan and plot the likelihood for all the POIs. For two POIs, I can use the plot2D_scan.py in order to draw the likelihood, but there are ~25 POIs for only one of the fits. Therefore, in order to check the scan of the likelihood, I was considering to project the scan to each POI separately. Could you please let me know, if there is an effective way to do it?
Thank you,
Giorgos
posts.cooked_html:
Dear Experts,

for the BPH-24-005, I am working on some fits with many POIs that are all measured simultaneously. I am trying to scan and plot the likelihood for all the POIs. For two POIs, I can use the plot2D_scan.py in order to draw the likelihood, but there are ~25 POIs for only one of the fits. Therefore, in order to check the scan of the likelihood, I was considering to project the scan to each POI separately. Could you please let me know, if there is an effective way to do it?

Thank you,
Giorgos

posts.name: Aliya Nigamova
posts.text:
Dear @gmelachr,
Are you sure that you are running 25-dimensional likelihood scan?
I would assume that while you are varying one POI, the rest of them are profiled like any other parameter in the model.
To do this you can run the scan for each parameter ( `–floatOtherPOIs=1` profiles all other POIs) and plot it with the following command:
```
combineTool.py -M MultiDimFit --datacard <ws.root> -t -1 --setParameters <poi_1>=<initial_value>,<poi_2>=<initial_value2>,…  --redefineSignalPOIs  --algo=grid --points=100 -P <poi_name> --floatOtherPOIs=1 -n .<poi_name>
python scripts/plot1DScan.py higgsCombinescan_.<poi_name>.MultiDimFit.mH120.root -o <poi_name> --POI  <poi_name>
```
Best,
Aliya
posts.cooked_html:
Dear @gmelachr,

Are you sure that you are running 25-dimensional likelihood scan?
I would assume that while you are varying one POI, the rest of them are profiled like any other parameter in the model.

To do this you can run the scan for each parameter ( `–floatOtherPOIs=1` profiles all other POIs) and plot it with the following command:

```
combineTool.py -M MultiDimFit --datacard <ws.root> -t -1 --setParameters <poi_1>=<initial_value>,<poi_2>=<initial_value2>,… --redefineSignalPOIs --algo=grid --points=100 -P <poi_name> --floatOtherPOIs=1 -n .<poi_name>

python scripts/plot1DScan.py higgsCombinescan_.<poi_name>.MultiDimFit.mH120.root -o <poi_name> --POI <poi_name>
```

Best,
Aliya

posts.text:

Thank you for your answer. I did it and I also broke down the uncertainty.
